name: Ragdoll
description: A character skeleton transitions from authored animation to simulated rigid bodies connected by joint constraints.
design_notes: Ragdoll is a transition between animation authority and physics authority. The practical decisions are skeletal body setup, collision layers, joint limits, hit impulse transfer, get-up pose selection, and whether death or knockdown state is authoritative for networking and save files.
design_purpose: Make impacts, deaths, knockdowns, or falls feel physically responsive.
Communicate loss of control through body motion rather than only animation playback.
Create emergent comedy, horror, or tactical cover interactions when bodies collide with the world.
player_fantasy: The player sees bodies react believably to impacts and understands when control or life has been lost.

parameters.name: skeletal_body_profile_id
parameters.description: Physics asset profile that defines body shapes, masses, and limb collision behavior.

parameters.name: joint_limit_profile_id
parameters.description: Constraint profile that limits joint rotation and stabilizes the simulated skeleton.

parameters.name: blend_in_ms
parameters.description: Milliseconds used to blend from animation pose into physics-driven limb motion.

parameters.name: impact_impulse_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to hit impulse before it is distributed to the affected body or limb.

parameters.name: getup_pose_selector_id
parameters.description: Rule used to choose an animation pose when a non-death ragdoll returns to controlled motion.

edge_cases: A character ragdolls while attached to a ladder, vehicle, moving platform, or cinematic root-motion track.
The body falls asleep inside a door, elevator, or other moving collision volume.
A get-up attempt begins when the pelvis is off navmesh or the selected pose intersects geometry.
Multiplayer clients simulate cosmetic ragdolls differently from the server-authoritative death state.
common_bugs: Animation keeps writing bone transforms after physics simulation starts, causing jitter or exploding joints.
Collision layers let limbs collide with the owning capsule or weapon and launch the body.
Joint constraints are too loose or solver iterations too low, producing stretching, spinning, or folded bodies.
Death state, loot spawn, and ragdoll cleanup run independently and despawn bodies before interactions finish.
balancing_notes: Long ragdoll recovery removes player agency; use it deliberately for heavy hits or readable punishment.
Combat readability often benefits from capped impact impulses so bodies do not obscure targets or objectives.
Temporary ragdolls need clear recovery rules or they can feel like random loss of control.
accessibility_notes: Offer reduced ragdoll intensity or less exaggerated impacts for players sensitive to violent body motion.
Keep critical gameplay information independent from ragdoll pose, since bodies may settle unpredictably.

implementation_notes.unity: Build ragdolls from configured Rigidbody and Joint components with collision layers that ignore the owning capsule. During transition, disable Animator writes or use a controlled blend, apply impact impulses once by attack_instance_id, and keep authoritative death state separate from cosmetic limb simulation.
implementation_notes.godot: PhysicalBone3D can drive skeletal physics, but state machines should explicitly switch animation, collision, and control modes. Store the skeleton root, sleeping state, and getup_pose_selector_id for knockdown recovery, and debug joint limits with visible constraint axes.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Physics Assets, Set Simulate Physics on skeletal bodies, Physical Animation Components for partial blends, and movement-mode changes for control loss. Networked games should replicate death or knockdown state and let clients simulate cosmetic ragdoll details within cleanup rules.
implementation_notes.web: For Three.js or custom engines, ragdoll is usually expensive; use simplified capsule chains or precomputed animation blends unless physics is central. If using a solver, keep stable body ids, joint anchors, and collision masks for rollback or replay captures.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto IV
example_games.note: Uses ragdoll-like body reactions prominently during impacts.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses physics-driven bodies as part of combat aftermath and world interaction.
